ops.text: A dev binary built from a clean tree serves a dead SPA: the only committed file under internal/web/dist is index.html, and it is a stale real build output referencing hashed assets (/assets/index-BrVms3HP.js, /assets/index-Cco7v25q.css) that were never committed — so go:embed compiles fine, the daemon serves the SPA shell, the asset request falls through to the HTML fallback (200 text/html for a .js module), and React never mounts with zero diagnostics anywhere. A browser-verification lane burned a full session proving the blank page wasn't a browser problem before finding the mismatch via curl -sI on the module URL. The AGENTS.md phrase "committed placeholder dist/index.html" reads as if the placeholder is inert; in fact it actively references dead hashes, and nothing (build tag, daemon startup check, or a genuinely inert placeholder without asset refs) warns that a clean-tree binary needs `bunx vite build` first. Either commit a truly inert placeholder index.html with no asset script tags, or have the daemon log a loud warning when an embedded index.html references assets the embed FS lacks.
